description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages install, test, analysis, external, build, and deploy using node:10-alpine as the default image, node:8-alpine for LTS jobs, and electrostar/sonar-scanner:latest for SonarQube. In the install stage define install:latest and install:lts jobs that run `npm run install:ci`, cache the `node_modules/` directory with key `$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG-$CI_JOB_NAME`, store `node_modules/` as a 1‑day artifact, and run only on the master and develop branches. In the test stage add test:latest and test:lts jobs that run `npm run test:ci`, produce a `coverage/` artifact (expire 1 day), depend on their respective install jobs, and are limited to master and develop. In the analysis stage add coverage (`npx coverage-average ./coverage/text-summary.txt`), docs:dev (`npm run docs:ci`), docs:prod (`npm run docs:ci:prod`), tslint (`npm run lint:ci`), and sonarqube (using `sonar-scanner -X -D sonar.host.url=$SONAR_URL -D sonar.login=$SONAR_TOKEN` after installing nodejs), each with appropriate only rules and dependencies. In the build stage add build:prod (`npm run build:prod` then `sed '/"devDependencies"/,/}/ d; /^$/d' -i dist/package.json` with a `dist/` artifact) and documentation (`npm run docs` with a `documentation/` artifact), both only on master. Finally, in the deploy stage add deploy:npm (install git via `apk add --no-cache git` then run `npx semantic-release` to publish to npm) and pages (copy documentation and coverage into a `public/` directory and publish it as a GitLab Pages artifact), both restricted to the master branch.
